mission.title: Install and Configure Bfe on Kubernetes
mission.description: BFE is a modern layer 7 load balancer from Baidu, designed for efficient traffic management and routing. Installing BFE allows you to leverage its capabilities for load balancing and traffic management in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Clone the BFE repository
steps.description:
First, clone the BFE repository to your local machine to access the necessary files for building Docker images.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/bfenetworks/bfe.git
cd bfe
```

steps.title: Build Docker images
steps.description:
Build the Docker images required for BFE. This command compiles the application and creates the necessary images.
```bash
make docker
```

steps.title: Deploy BFE to Kubernetes
steps.description:
Apply the Kubernetes manifests to deploy BFE in your cluster. Ensure you have the necessary YAML files in the repository.
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/
```

steps.title: Verify BFE deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the BFE pods to ensure they are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace default
```

uninstall.title: Remove BFE deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the BFE deployment from your Kubernetes cluster to cleanly uninstall it.
```bash
kubectl delete -f deploy/kubernetes/
```

uninstall.title: Clean up remaining resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any remaining custom resources or configurations related to BFE.
```bash
kubectl delete crd bfeconfigs.bfenetworks.com
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace (if created)
uninstall.description:
If you created a specific namespace for BFE, delete it to clean up completely.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace bfe-namespace
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of the BFE deployment.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace default -o yaml > bfe-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the BFE repository to get the newest version.
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Rebuild Docker images
upgrade.description:
Rebuild the Docker images with the latest changes.
```bash
make docker
```

upgrade.title: Apply updated manifests
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated Kubernetes manifests to upgrade the deployment.
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/
```

upgrade.title: Verify upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the BFE pods to ensure the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace default
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your BFE pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace default
```

troubleshooting.title: BFE pods crashing
troubleshooting.description:
If BFE pods are crashing, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace default
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the BFE service is not reachable, verify the service configuration and endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace default
kubectl describe svc bfe --namespace default
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of BFE will result in running pods that are healthy and responding to traffic. You should be able to access the BFE service through the configured endpoints.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed.
